databases.seed_sql:
CREATE TABLE crew (
  crew_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  callsign TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL,
  status TEXT NOT NULL,
  pod_id INTEGER
);
CREATE TABLE pods (
  pod_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  deck TEXT NOT NULL,
  occupied INTEGER NOT NULL
);
INSERT INTO pods (pod_id, deck, occupied) VALUES
  (1, 'A', 1),
  (2, 'A', 1),
  (3, 'B', 1),
  (4, 'B', 0),
  (5, 'C', 1),
  (6, 'C', 1);
INSERT INTO crew (crew_id, callsign, role, status, pod_id) VALUES
  (1, 'ORION', 'navigator', 'active', 1),
  (2, 'MICA', 'systems', 'active', 2),
  (3, 'KELL', 'medic', 'missing', 3),
  (4, 'RIN', 'engineer', 'missing', 5),
  (5, 'VOSS', 'pilot', 'active', 6),
  (6, 'NYX', 'archivist', 'active', NULL);

hints.text: Open the schema browser. The crew table has status values.
Try SELECT callsign, role FROM crew WHERE status = 'missing' ORDER BY callsign;
A second query can return only callsign without ORDER BY.
The census assertion checks that SELECT-only policy left six crew rows intact.

objectives.description: Select missing crew callsigns and roles, ordered by callsign.
Select the missing callsigns (order does not matter).
Confirm the crew census still has six seeded rows.

summary: Query the isolated crew registry to find records marked missing.
title: Missing Crew
